title: Sliding Window — Dry Run & Recall
oneSentence: Trace Maximum Average Subarray, Max Vowels, and Minimum Window Substring.

maxAvgSubarray.title: Maximum Average Subarray I
maxAvgSubarray.description: Find a contiguous subarray of size k with the maximum average value.
maxAvgSubarray.story: A magnifying glass of width K slides across the array. Add right, remove left, track max sum.
code: public static double solve(int[] nums, int k) {
double windowSum = 0, maxSum = 0;
// STEP 1 — BUILD: Place the magnifying glass at the start — sum up the first K elements
for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) windowSum += nums[i];
maxSum = windowSum; // first window is our initial best
// STEP 2 — SLIDE: Move the glass one step at a time
for (int i = k; i < nums.length; i++) {
windowSum += nums[i];    // new number enters from the right → add it
windowSum -= nums[i - k]; // old number exits from the left → subtract it
// STEP 3 — TRACK: Is this new window better than our best?
maxSum = Math.max(maxSum, windowSum);
// STEP 4 — RETURN: Convert the best sum to average
return maxSum / k;

steps.desc: Build initial window (0 to 3): 1 + 12 + (-5) + (-6) = 2. maxSum = 2.
Slide index 4: Add nums[4] (50) to windowSum (2 + 50 = 52). Deduct nums[0] (1) -> windowSum becomes 51.
Track: Math.max(maxSum 2, newSum 51) = 51.
Slide index 5: Add nums[5] (3) -> (51 + 3 = 54). Deduct nums[1] (12) -> windowSum becomes 42.
Track: Math.max(maxSum 51, newSum 42) = 51.
Loop finishes. Return maxSum / k = 51.0 / 4 = 12.75.

quiz.q: In Maximum Average Subarray I (Fixed Window), how do we efficiently slide the window of size K?
options: Add the new element on the right and subtract the element exiting on the left.
Recalculate the sum of all K elements from scratch at each step.
Double the window size whenever the average is negative.
quiz.explain: To maintain O(N) time complexity, we slide the window by adding the entering element and subtracting the exiting element, updating the sum in O(1) time.

maxVowels.title: Max Vowels of Length K
maxVowels.description: Given s and k, return the max number of vowels in any substring of length k.
maxVowels.story: Magnifying glass, but instead of summing numbers, count vowels. Add right, remove left.
code: public static int solve(String s, int k) {
int vowelCount = 0, maxVowels = 0;
// STEP 1 — BUILD: Count vowels in the first window of size K
for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) if (isVowel(s.charAt(i))) vowelCount++;
maxVowels = vowelCount;
// STEP 2 — SLIDE: Move the window one step at a time
for (int i = k; i < s.length(); i++) {
if (isVowel(s.charAt(i))) vowelCount++;      // vowel enters from right → count up
if (isVowel(s.charAt(i - k))) vowelCount--;  // vowel exits from left → count down
// STEP 3 — TRACK: Is this window better than our best?
maxVowels = Math.max(maxVowels, vowelCount);
// STEP 4 — RETURN
return maxVowels;
private static boolean isVowel(char c) { return c=='a'||c=='e'||c=='i'||c=='o'||c=='u'; }

steps.desc: Build initial window 'abc': vowelCount = 1 ('a'). maxVowels = 1.
Slide index 3 ('i'): 'i' enters (is vowel -> vowelCount becomes 2). 'a' (index 0) exits (is vowel -> vowelCount becomes 1).
Track: Math.max(1, 1) = 1.
Slide index 4 ('i'): 'i' enters (is vowel -> vowelCount becomes 2). 'b' (index 1) exits (not vowel -> count stays 2).
Track: Math.max(1, 2) = 2.
Slide index 5 ('i'): 'i' enters (is vowel -> vowelCount becomes 3). 'c' (index 2) exits (not vowel -> count stays 3).
Track: Math.max(2, 3) = 3.
Slide index 6 ('d'): 'd' enters (not vowel). 'i' (index 3) exits (is vowel -> vowelCount decrements to 2).
Track: Math.max(3, 2) = 3. Return 3.

quiz.q: When sliding the window in Max Vowels of Length K, how do we update the vowel count?
options: Increment if the entering character is a vowel, and decrement if the exiting character is a vowel.
Re-count all vowels in the current window of size K at each step.
Vowel counts cannot be updated dynamically and must be re-evaluated.
quiz.explain: By checking only the entering and exiting characters, we update the vowel count in O(1) time instead of O(K).

longestNoRepeat.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating
longestNoRepeat.description: Find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
longestNoRepeat.story: Bouncer at the door: If duplicate enters, kick leftmost copy out until club has no duplicates.
code: public static int solve(String s) {
// STEP 1 — INIT: Set up the window tracking and pointer variables
Set<Character> window = new HashSet<>();
int left = 0, maxLen = 0;
// STEP 2 — EXPAND: Move the right pointer to expand the window
for (int right = 0; right < s.length(); right++) {
char current = s.charAt(right);
// STEP 3 — SHRINK: If duplicate exists, shrink left pointer until duplicate is removed
while (window.contains(s.charAt(right))) {
window.remove(s.charAt(left));
left++; // shrink from left
// STEP 4 — ADD: It's now safe to add the current character to the window
window.add(s.charAt(right));
// STEP 5 — TRACK: The window is guaranteed valid (no duplicates) — update max length
maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, right - left + 1);
// STEP 6 — RETURN
return maxLen;

steps.desc: Init: left=0, right starts before array. window is empty. maxLen=0.
right=0 ('a'): 'a' not in window. Add 'a'. window={a}. maxLen=max(0,1)=1.
right=1 ('b'): 'b' not in window. Add 'b'. window={a,b}. maxLen=max(1,2)=2.
right=2 ('c'): 'c' not in window. Add 'c'. window={a,b,c}. maxLen=max(2,3)=3.
right=3 ('a'): 'a' IS in window! Enter shrink-while loop.
Shrink: remove s[0]='a'. left becomes 1. window={b,c}. 'a' gone — exit shrink loop.
Add 'a'. window={b,c,a}. maxLen=max(3,3)=3.
right=4 ('b'): 'b' IS in window. Enter shrink loop.
Shrink: remove s[1]='b'. left=2. window={c,a}. 'b' gone.
Add 'b'. window={c,a,b}. maxLen=max(3,3)=3.
right=5 ('c'): 'c' IS in window. Enter shrink loop.
Shrink: remove s[2]='c'. left=3. window={a,b}.
Add 'c'. window={a,b,c}. maxLen=max(3,3)=3.
right=6 ('b'): 'b' IS in window. Enter shrink loop.
Shrink: remove 'a'(left=3)->left=4. Still 'b' in window. Remove 'b'(left=4)->left=5. window={c}.
Add 'b'. window={c,b}. maxLen=max(3,2)=3.
right=7 ('b'): 'b' IS in window. Shrink: remove s[5]='c'->left=6, then s[6]='b'->left=7.
Add 'b'. window={b}. maxLen=max(3,1)=3. Loop ends. Return 3! ✅

quiz.q: In a variable sliding window for finding the LONGEST valid substring, when do we update our max length?
options: Directly after the while-shrink loop, once the window is confirmed to be valid (no duplicates).
Inside the while-shrink loop while duplicates still exist.
At the beginning of the outer loop before adding the new character.
quiz.explain: We record the length after shrinking the window to a valid state, representing the longest valid window ending at the current right pointer.

minWindowSubstring.title: Minimum Window Substring
minWindowSubstring.description: Given s and t, return the minimum window substring of s containing all characters of t.
minWindowSubstring.story: Shopping list matching. Expand right until valid, shrink left to trim off excess, track shortest valid.
code: public static String solve(String s, String t) {
// STEP 1 — INIT: Build the shopping list from t
Map<Character, Integer> need = new HashMap<>();
for (char c : t.toCharArray()) need.put(c, need.getOrDefault(c, 0) + 1);
int required = need.size(); // how many UNIQUE chars must be fully matched
int matched = 0;            // how many unique chars ARE fully matched now
// STEP 2 — INIT: Set up the window tracking
Map<Character, Integer> have = new HashMap<>();
int bestLen = Integer.MAX_VALUE, bestStart = 0, left = 0;
// STEP 3 — EXPAND: Widen the telescope to the right, one character at a time
for (int right = 0; right < s.length(); right++) {
char r = s.charAt(right);
have.put(r, have.getOrDefault(r, 0) + 1);
// STEP 4 — CHECK: Did adding this char complete a shopping list requirement?
if (need.containsKey(r) && have.get(r).intValue() == need.get(r).intValue()) matched++; // one more requirement satisfied
// STEP 5 — SHRINK: While ALL shopping list items are found → try shrinking from left
while (matched == required) {
// STEP 6 — TRACK: This is a valid window! Is it shorter than our best?
if (right - left + 1 < bestLen) {
bestLen = right - left + 1; bestStart = left;
// Remove the leftmost character from the window
char l = s.charAt(left);
have.put(l, have.get(l) - 1);
// Did removing this char break a shopping list requirement?
if (need.containsKey(l) && have.get(l).intValue() < need.get(l).intValue()) matched--; // lost a requirement → loop will exit
left++; // shrink window from left
// STEP 7 — RETURN: Return the shortest valid window, or empty if none found
return bestLen == Integer.MAX_VALUE ? "" : s.substring(bestStart, bestStart + bestLen);

steps.desc: R=0 ('A'): Add 'A' to have. 'A' matched count! matched=1.
R=1..3: Add 'D', 'O', 'B'. 'B' matched count! matched=2 (need A,B,C).
R=4..5: Add 'E', 'C'. 'C' matched! matched=3 (matched == required!). Enter shrink loop.
Shrink L=0: remove 'A'. 'A' count drops below need! matched=2. Exit shrink loop. left becomes 1.
R=6..10: Add 'O','D','E','B','A'. 'A' found again, matched=3! Enter shrink loop. Current length = 10.
Shrink L=1..4: remove 'D','O','B','E' (they are not bottleneck). left=5. Window 'CODEBA' length = 6. Still valid.
Shrink L=5: remove 'C'. 'C' drops below need! matched=2. left=6. Exit shrink.
R=11..12: Add 'N','C'. 'C' matches, matched=3! Enter shrink loop.
Shrink L=6..8: remove excess 'O','D','E'. left=9. Window is 'BANC' length = 4! Update bestLen = 4.
Shrink L=9: remove 'B'. matched=2. left=10. Loop ends. Return 'BANC'.

quiz.q: In Minimum Window Substring, why do we shrink the window from the left as soon as we have a valid window (matched == required)?
options: To find if a smaller valid window exists by trimming off excess characters from the left.
To make the window invalid so we can expand it further.
To reset the character map and start a new search.
quiz.explain: Once the window is valid, we attempt to shrink it from the left to find the minimum possible length that still contains all required characters.
